trivia_bullets.text: Nadir Kraken originated in Theros Beyond Death (released 2020-01-24), a set themed around the Underworld of Theros — the realm below the mortal plane ruled by Erebos. The 'nadir' (lowest point) in the card name ties directly to the set's descent-into-death framing: the card's name is literal (krakens dwell in the deepest ocean) and puns on THB's Underworld theme. [Scryfall; WebSearch snippet: "nadir means lowest point"]

The Murders at Karlov Manor Commander (2024) printing carries flavor text: "Meeting one is definitely the low point of your life." The pun — 'low point' echoing 'nadir' — is the card's signature wit. Earlier printings including the NCC copy in inventory have no flavor text. [Scryfall API: MKC/112]

Nadir Kraken appears in 57,508 Commander decks tracked by EDHREC (EDHREC rank 2516), making it a widely played draw-reward creature. Its ability triggers on every draw — including opponents' draws in group-hug builds — and the optional {1} payment lets pilots modulate how much mana they sink into growth. The card generates both +1/+1 counters on itself AND 1/1 Tentacle tokens simultaneously, creating a self-scaling threat plus a token army from a single source. Commander Spellbook documents over 40 known infinite combos involving Nadir Kraken, pairing it with Ashnod's Altar and draw engines. [EDHREC meta description via WebSearch; Scryfall]

Draftsim's ranking of all krakens in Magic places Nadir Kraken at number 9 out of 29, calling it 'perhaps the most value-oriented kraken in Magic.' The NCC copy in inventory is a reprint (originally THB); subsequent reprintings include March of the Machine Commander (2023), Murders at Karlov Manor Commander (2024), and Foundations Jumpstart (2024) — at least 8 total printings across paper and digital. Artist for this NCC printing is Dan Murayama Scott. [Draftsim: All 29 Krakens Ranked, via WebSearch; Scryfall: prints search]
